channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft leaders meet with Trump, Musk at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Microsoft’s top leaders met with President-elect Trump, Vice President-elect JD Vance and Elon Musk at Mar-a-Lago on Wednesday, a company spokesperson confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Schumer pushes for TikTok ban delay as deadline looms
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) on Thursday called for a looming TikTok ban to be delayed to give the social media company more time to find an American buyer. "We aren’t against TikTok. We want TikTok to keep going.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Paraglider Forced to Make Emergency Landing After Drone Collision
item.description: A paraglider in California was forced to make an emergency landing after his parachute became entangled with a drone, highlighting the risks of irresponsible drone operation.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, NewsNation

item.title: Surprise finding sheds light on what causes Huntington's disease, a devastating fatal brain disorder
item.description: Scientists are unraveling the mystery of what triggers Huntington’s disease, a devastating hereditary disorder that strikes in the prime of life.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AirPods Pro’s long-promised heart-rate monitoring could be one step closer – but don’t wait up for it
item.description: A tidbit in a new report might send pulses racing, but the roadmap to AirPods with an actual, reliable ticker-taker seems to be a long and winding one.
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Substack is trying to court displaced TikTok creators
item.description: “We’re going to rescue the smart people from TikTok!” Substack CEO Chris Best wrote in a Note posted Monday. If you’ve been on your Substack newsfeed lately, you’ve likely seen the platform rolling out a warm welcome to TikTok users, as the days count down to a potential TikTok ban in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Redefining enterprise transformation in the age of intelligent ecosystems
item.description: As IT professionals and business decision-makers, we’ve routinely used the term “digital transformation” for well over a decade now to describe a portfolio of enterprise initiatives that somehow magically enable strategic business capabilities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Soft robotic armband gives prosthetic hand users natural control
item.description: Researchers found in a study that users of a robotic prosthetic hand could successfully grasp and move two objects at once without dropping them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Kazakhstan’s AI-Powered Prisons: Setting a New Standard for Safety and Efficiency
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: H-1B visa: South Bay executives must be re-charged with visa fraud, judges rule
item.description: Federal prosecutors claimed the two falsely stated in 85 visa applications that prospective H-1B holders would work on-site on internal projects at their San Jose computer chip business, PerfectVIPs.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Mac malware mayhem as 100 million Apple users at risk of having personal data stolen
item.description: New Mac malware variant targets browser credentials and personal data, researchers find. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives tips to safeguard your Mac from the latest malware threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 ways to avoid AI in Windows
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) is seemingly everywhere these days — and Windows is certainly no exception. The OS itself now has Copilot on its taskbar, and new PCs ship with a dedicated Copilot AI key on their keyboards.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications
item.description: The Homeland Security Department's Science and Technology Directorate has an ongoing research program for the development of cellular. The post Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers claim massive breach of company that tracks and sells Americans' location data
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals that hackers stole data from Gravy Analytics, a major seller of smartphone location data to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shape-shifting AI Transformer homes will leave you wanting one
item.description: AC Future and Pininfarina unveil AI Transformer homes, merging sustainability and innovation. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this could change the way we think about home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HPE Aruba Networking Rolls Out New Products for Retailers
item.description: HPE Aruba Networking has unveiled a range of new products designed to boost connectivity, improve analytics, and streamline management for retailers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 800,000 VW electric vehicle owners' data exposed by software bug
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on a Volkswagen data leak that exposed the personal data of around 800,000 EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: The top five procurement themes for 2025
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base. The post The top five procurement themes for 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shutdown
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World
